app.name: Junkyard Arcade
app.tagline: Turn meme dust into verifiable blind boxes.

home.title: Junkyard Arcade
home.subtitle: Ape into verifiable onchain blind boxes.
home.step1Desc: Creators define sale parameters, guards, oracle, and max asset types per opening before funding the prize pool.
home.step2Desc: Users buy a soulbound blind box and pay oracle cost only when they decide to open it.
home.step3Desc: Once VRF resolves, prizes are claimable, the box is burned, and empty pools wait for creator restocks.
home.story1Title: Turn wallet leftovers into something people actually want to open.
home.story2Title: The box stays unresolved until randomness lands onchain.
home.story3Title: Run the whole drop from one readable control surface.
home.faq1Question: What actually lives inside a Junkyard Arcade blind box?
home.faq1Answer: Each series can be funded with ERC20, ERC721, and ERC1155 rewards. What you receive depends on the prize pool that the creator configured for that specific drop.
home.faq2Question: Why is opening separated from buying?
home.faq2Answer: Buying mints the blind box NFT. Opening requests randomness. That split keeps the reveal hidden until the oracle responds and makes the outcome verifiable onchain.
home.faq3Question: Can creators keep managing a drop after launch?
home.faq3Answer: Yes. Creators can keep funding rewards, update sale parameters within protocol rules, monitor whitelist state, and handle leftovers after the drop finishes.
home.faq4Question: Do I need to trust an offchain reveal server?
home.faq4Answer: No. The reveal path is designed around Chainlink VRF, so the randomness and final reward flow are resolved through onchain interactions instead of a private reveal script.

docs.sidebarDescription: Single-page documentation index covering the project overview, highlights, and usage instructions.
docs.heroDescription: This page organizes the blind box protocol into a more traditional documentation reading experience, covering overview, core features, and operational usage.

pages.title: Project Introduction
pages.description: Junkyard Arcade is a full-stack onchain blind box system built around verifiable randomness, multi-asset prize pools, and an operator-friendly web console.

blocks.content: The protocol packages token dust, NFTs, and mixed inventories into blind box series. Creators launch a series contract, fund a prize pool, and let collectors buy, open, and claim through a single product surface.
The repository is split into three layers: Foundry contracts for protocol rules, a Graph subgraph for indexed state, and a Next.js 15 frontend for the operator and collector experience.

items: Contracts: Solidity + Foundry for factory, series, prize pool, and oracle integrations.
Indexer: The Graph subgraph for series, blind box, asset, and transaction state.
Frontend: Next.js 15 App Router, React 19, wagmi, RainbowKit, shadcn/ui.

blocks.content: The current creator flow is intentionally constrained: Base Sepolia only, preset factory/oracle wiring, and a UI that optimizes clarity over protocol breadth.

pages.title: Quick Start
pages.description: The shortest path to run the workspace locally and validate that contracts, subgraph, and frontend are wired together.

blocks.content: Copy the example env for the web app and fill in the subgraph endpoint plus Base Sepolia factory/oracle presets.

blocks.code:
pnpm --dir apps/contracts test
pnpm --dir apps/subgraph codegen
pnpm --dir apps/subgraph build

pnpm --dir apps/web graphclient:build
pnpm --dir apps/web dev

pages.title: Core Features
pages.description: The protocol is opinionated in the places where launch mistakes are expensive and flexible where creators need room to design drops.

blocks.content: Blind boxes remain unresolved until the oracle fulfills randomness. That keeps prize assignment hidden before opening and removes the need for offchain reveal scripts.

items: ERC1155 balances for semi-fungible prize buckets.

blocks.content: Creators can update sale settings within protocol limits, configure leftover handling, and manage reward inventory from the admin dock on the series detail page.

pages.title: Blind Box Lifecycle
pages.description: From deployment to leftover cleanup, the protocol separates each stage so the system stays auditable and operationally clear.

blocks.content: The factory creates a new series contract with the chosen name, symbol, supply cap, sale window, payment asset, and guard/oracle presets.
The series owner initializes reward assets, whitelists them, approves transfers, and deposits inventory into the prize pool before the drop goes live.
Collectors purchase a blind box NFT, request randomness to open it, and claim after fulfillment resolves the prize outcome.
After the series ends and claims settle, the owner can execute the configured leftover strategy against remaining assets.

pages.title: Creator Workflow
pages.description: How a creator launches, funds, monitors, and closes a blind box series using the current frontend.

items: Open /create and connect the creator wallet.
Fill name, symbol, supply cap, price, and sale window.
Submit the deployment transaction and wait for redirect to the new series detail page.

blocks.content: Use the admin tabs on the series detail page to whitelist reward assets and deposit ERC20, ERC721, or ERC1155 inventory.
Track sales and claims from /series and the series detail dashboard. If needed, tighten the sale window or apply leftover policy after the campaign ends.

pages.title: Collector Workflow
pages.description: How a player discovers a drop, buys a box, opens it, and claims the final reward.

blocks.content: Browse /series to compare active drops, supply pressure, and sale windows before entering a specific series.
Open the target series detail page, connect a wallet, and purchase from the action dock. The box is minted directly into the connected wallet.
Use the owned blind box shelf on the series detail page or /me to request opening. Return after fulfillment to claim the reward and close the box lifecycle.

pages.title: Configuration Reference
pages.description: The minimal environment values required for the current Base Sepolia deployment path and frontend experience.

items: NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL: canonical host for metadata and sitemap.
NEXT_PUBLIC_FACTORY_SEPOLIA: factory preset used by the create flow.
NEXT_PUBLIC_DEFAULT_ORACLE_SEPOLIA: default Chainlink VRF preset exposed in the create form.

blocks.content: The frontend create flow is intentionally fixed to Base Sepolia today. If production networks are added later, this page should expand alongside the preset system.

create.title: Create Series
create.overviewCard1Desc: Define identity, supply, sale window, and payment mode before the first mint ever happens.
create.overviewCard2Desc: Guards and whitelisted contracts decide who can configure, buy, and request randomness.
create.pageDesc: Launch a new blind box series on Base Sepolia from your wallet.
create.desc: The create flow is fixed to one supported network with readable presets for payment, guards, and oracle.
create.formDesc: Review identity, sale settings, and contract presets before sending the deployment transaction.
create.sectionIdentityDesc: These values define how the soulbound blind box collection will appear onchain and in wallets.
create.sectionSaleDesc: Configure price, supply, opening limits, and time window with immutable constraints in mind.
create.sectionSecurityDesc: Attach the oracle and both guard contracts that protect configuration and purchase flows.
create.name: NFT name
create.maxAssetTypesPerOpeningHint: Immutable after creation. Controls how many distinct asset contracts one box can distribute.
create.endTimeHint: Leave empty to keep the drop open-ended.
create.previewDesc: A compact read-through before you sign.
create.checklistDesc: The critical rules that are easiest to miss when you're moving fast.
create.check1: Price is entered in ether and converted to the onchain wei amount at submit time.
create.check2: Max asset types per opening is fixed at creation time and cannot be edited later.
create.check3: Leaving the end date empty keeps the sale open until you close or reconfigure it onchain.
create.quickStatusDesc: Three fast signals before the wallet popup.

funding.connectDesc: Connect the creator wallet before configuring reward rails or topping up balances.

actions.connectPrompt: Wallet needed to operate this series
actions.connectDesc: Connect first to buy boxes, request randomness, or claim a resolved reward.
actions.openDesc: Submit an open request for this box. After the transaction confirms, Chainlink still needs time to fulfill randomness.
actions.waitingDesc: The randomness request has been sent and is still pending Chainlink fulfillment. Claim becomes available automatically once it resolves.
actions.claimDesc: Randomness has resolved. You can now claim the final reward.

admin.title: Basic info config
admin.panelDesc: Sale controls and leftover policy live here. Use this dock when the series needs tightening, extension, or final cleanup.
admin.connectTip: Connect wallet to manage this series (guard rules apply).
admin.recipientHint: For BURN, recipient will be ignored by contract. For DONATE, recipient cannot be zero.
admin.sweepHint: Sweep leftovers after series ends and all boxes are claimed.

leftovers.title: Leftover Sweeps
leftovers.desc: Asset-level records for prize pool leftovers swept after the series ends.

seriesPage.loadFailedDesc: Subgraph request failed or no data returned.

me.title: My blind-box deck
me.desc: Track what is still sealed, what already resolved, and which series need your next action before value sits idle.
me.connectHint: Connect a wallet to load your box rack and action queue.
me.metricOwnedHint: Every blind box currently tied to this wallet.
me.metricQueueHint: Boxes still waiting to be opened or claimed.
me.metricSeriesHint: Distinct active series still worth monitoring.
me.queueDesc: The next boxes most likely to need your attention, surfaced before they disappear into the full rack.
me.queueEmpty: Nothing is waiting on you right now. Your queue is clear.

me.connectDesc: Connect the wallet that purchased your blind boxes to load the rack, the pending queue, and each series shortcut.
